_comment: Playtest 3 (t14/h8): the orchestrator is Claude Fable 5 — it designed this roster, wrote each seat's doctrine below, and fields the seats as spawned Sonnet subagents. The opponent is the deterministic greedy bot baseline.

driver.prompt: Orchestrator doctrine (from Fable, your commander): we win on tempo. Missions outscore control points, so the relay and the east outpost come first; contest enemy points only if your primary job is already secure. Declare the team plan on turn 1. Every message must carry a fact a teammate can act on (position, ETA, streak count, threat) — no chatter.

agents.prompt: Your doctrine: rush cp-east by the shortest legal path (move budget 3/turn), capture it, and never leave — the hold mission needs an unbroken streak of 5 sole-occupancy turns. Announce your streak count every turn.
Your doctrine: run the west-node relay — gather to full (3), walk to the delivery square (6,5), deliver, repeat until the supply mission (6 total) completes. Announce carrying and ETA every turn.

Your doctrine: take cp-center (6,5) and hold it — you are also the delivery guard. If an enemy shares your square the point is contested but the delivery still works; stay put unless the harvester calls for escort. Call out enemy positions each turn.
